summary: Matched a batch of previously 0% menu-system functions, mostly under src/melee/mn. The largest additions implemented mnevent, mnitemsw, mncount, and mnmainrule functions and replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete prototypes and local data structs. A cross-cutting change retyped mn_8022EC18 from float* animation loop triples to AnimLoopSettings*, then updated menu callers accordingly. decomp.dev reported 15 new 100% matches and +2768 matched bytes; the only human review feedback explicitly agreed that AnimLoopSettings made sense.

key_files.reason: Added the bulk of event menu decompilation, including mnEvent_8024CE74, mnEvent_8024D014, mnEvent_8024D0CC, mnEvent_8024D4E0, mnEvent_8024D7E0, fn_8024E1B4, fn_8024E2A0, fn_8024E34C, mnEvent_8024E420, and mnEvent_8024E838.
Introduced MnEventData with HSD_GObj/HSD_Text arrays and replaced many placeholder prototypes with typed signatures and externs.
Changed mn_8022EC18 to take AnimLoopSettings* and use start_frame/end_frame/loop_frame field accesses.
Updated the public prototype for mn_8022EC18 to AnimLoopSettings*.
Matched mnCount_802513F4 and mnCount_802517E0 using MnCountData, JObj visibility toggles, and AnimLoopSettings.
Added MnCountData layout and typed mnCount function prototypes.
Added item switch menu functions mnItemSw_8023405C and mnItemSw_802358C0.
Created header guard, MnItemSwData layout, typed prototypes, and mnItemSw_804D6BEC extern.
Matched mn_8023164C, mn_80231714, and mn_80231F80; added rule menu state transition logic and gm_1A3F dependency.
Converted f32[3] animation triples to AnimLoopSettings and updated field access.
Updated mn_8022EC18 calls to pass whole AnimLoopSettings objects rather than start_frame addresses.
Adjusted existing vector-like animation data to mn_8022EC18's new AnimLoopSettings* API via casts.
Converted mnVibration_803EECF8 from f32[3] to AnimLoopSettings.

what_changed: Implemented a large portion of src/melee/mn/mnevent.c, which had previously been effectively empty in the diff slice.
Added MnEventData with page, first_event, gobjs[9], texts[9], icons[9], name_text, and desc_text fields, enabling typed access to event menu state.

Implemented event menu lifecycle and animation helpers, including visibility toggling for navigation arrows, description text creation/freeing, exit animation cleanup, event-page initialization, and archive section loading in mnEvent_8024E838.
Implemented mnEvent_8024CE74 as the event-unlock/count threshold helper; decomp.dev still reported it as 99.42%, so it was improved but not fully matched in this PR.
Added MnItemSwData and implemented mnItemSw_8023405C for locating item switch JObjs from two child chains split at index 16.
Implemented mnItemSw_802358C0 as an item switch menu entry setup routine that updates mn_804A04F0, initializes item switch state, and installs fn_80233E10 as a GObj proc.
Added MnCountData and matched mnCount_802513F4, which shows/hides count menu arrow JObjs based on cursor and then applies mnCount_803EFAA0 animation settings.
Matched mnCount_802517E0, initializing cursor, page_size, label/value text pointers, and related menu data.
Matched mnmainrule functions mn_8023164C, mn_80231714, and mn_80231F80, replacing stub comments and placeholder signatures with real menu transition/rule availability logic.
Retyped mn_8022EC18 from float* to AnimLoopSettings* and updated users in mnmain.h, mndeflicker.c, mnhyaku.c, mnlanguage.c, mnsound.c, and mnvibration.c.
Converted several f32[3] animation-loop triples to named AnimLoopSettings values, notably mnHyaku_803EF668, mnHyaku_803EF674, and mnVibration_803EECF8.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations in headers with concrete return types and arguments involving HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, HSD_Text, Vec3, s32, u8, and bool.
decomp.dev reported +0.07% matched code overall, +2768 matched bytes, and 15 new 100% matches across mnevent, mnmainrule, mncount, and mnitemsw.
smart_moves: Recognized that repeated three-float animation arrays represent AnimLoopSettings, making mn_8022EC18 self-documenting through start_frame, end_frame, and loop_frame fields.
Kept matching-sensitive behavior in mn_8022EC18 by preserving the volatile float load of start_frame when requesting animation.
Used explicit struct layouts with offsets and array sizes for MnCountData, MnEventData, and MnItemSwData, which reduced placeholder usage while documenting observed memory layout.
Used PAD_STACK and FORCE_PAD_STACK_4 in functions where the match required stack-shape padding.
Maintained decomp style by naming still-anonymous internal callbacks as fn_-address while giving known menu functions mnEvent/mnCount/mnItemSw prefixes.
When existing data was not yet retyped, used targeted casts to AnimLoopSettings* in mnlanguage.c rather than forcing a broader struct/data migration without evidence.
Updated callers and headers together after the mn_8022EC18 API change, avoiding mismatched prototypes across menu files.
Modeled menu cleanup paths explicitly by freeing HSD_Text with HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4 and unlinking GObjs with HSD_GObjPLink_80390228 before nulling arrays in mnevent cleanup.
decomp_lessons: Three contiguous floats used as animation start/end/loop values in menu code are strong candidates for AnimLoopSettings, especially when passed to mn_8022EC18 or mn_8022ED6C.
Changing a helper signature to a semantic struct pointer can unlock cleaner matches across many callers, but existing data may still need casts until its type is proven.
For menu data structs, pointer arrays to HSD_Text and HSD_GObj often reveal fixed list/page capacities; here MnEventData uses arrays of 9 and MnCountData uses arrays of 10.
JObj visibility toggles for menu arrows can be matched using lb_80011E24 followed by HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll/HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll with JOBJ_HIDDEN.
Menu entry functions commonly update mn_804A04F0.prev_menu, cur_menu, and hovered_selection, then create a GObj and attach a proc with HSD_GObj_SetupProc plus HSD_GObj_804D783C flags.
Archive section loading in menu init code may require treating a data-symbol address as a byte base and using offsets to reproduce the original relocation/data access pattern.
Even a nearly complete function should be recorded honestly: mnEvent_8024CE74 improved from 0% to 99.42%, not 100%, according to the automated report.
naming_conventions: Menu subsystem functions retain the module prefix plus address, e.g. mnEvent_8024E838, mnCount_802517E0, mnItemSw_8023405C.
Anonymous or local callback-like functions are kept as fn_address, e.g. fn_8024E1B4, fn_8024E2A0, fn_8024E34C.
Menu data structs use module-specific CamelCase names ending in Data: MnEventData, MnCountData, MnItemSwData.
Struct fields use descriptive names when inferred from behavior, such as cursor, page_size, labels, values, title, page, first_event, name_text, and desc_text.
Known HSD types are reflected directly in prototypes and fields: HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, HSD_Text, HSD_GObjProc, HSD_Archive.
Animation-loop globals converted to AnimLoopSettings preserve their original address-based symbol names, e.g. mnHyaku_803EF668 and mnVibration_803EECF8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), and FORCE_PAD_STACK_4 to satisfy stack layout requirements in newly matched menu functions.
Preserved volatile load semantics in mn_8022EC18 with *(volatile float*) &arg1->start_frame.
Used exact HSD_TypeMask constants at call sites, such as 0x80, 0x400, and MOBJ_MASK, to preserve behavior and matching.
Retyped f32[3] arrays to AnimLoopSettings where evidence was strong, then changed indexing arg1[0]/arg1[1]/arg1[2] into field accesses start_frame/end_frame/loop_frame.
Applied casts to AnimLoopSettings* for mnLanguage globals that still appeared as other float/vector-shaped data, minimizing churn while satisfying the new helper signature.
Used child/next JObj traversal via HSD_JObjGetChild and HSD_JObjGetNext in mnItemSw_8023405C to match indexed menu item lookup.
Used address-relative char* arithmetic in mnEvent_8024E838 for lbArchive_LoadSections arguments, suggesting a match tactic for data blobs whose typed asset structure is not fully known.
Replaced placeholder prototypes with exact argument and return types after function bodies made ABI usage clear.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.text: Author said they were taking a crack at remaining 0% files and were a little suspicious of the AnimLoopSettings changes.
Reported matched code 58.59% (+0.07%, +2768 bytes), 15 new matches, and 5 improvements in unmatched items.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish mnEvent_8024CE74 from 99.42% to 100%.
Search for remaining f32[3] animation-loop arrays passed to mn_8022EC18 or mn_8022ED6C that should become AnimLoopSettings.
Audit mnlanguage globals mnLanguage_803EF5AC and mnLanguage_803EF5A0 to determine whether casts can be replaced with real AnimLoopSettings types.
Search for menu structs with fixed HSD_Text or HSD_GObj arrays to reuse MnEventData and MnCountData layout patterns.
Look for later renames of fn_8024E1B4, fn_8024E2A0, fn_8024E34C, fn_8024D864, and other anonymous event-menu callbacks.
